page: Microsoft Azure FX4-2mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. This virtualized instance, managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, features 1 physical core supporting 2 vCPUs through hyperthreading. It is equipped with a substantial 260 MB L3 cache and 84.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 42.0 GB per core. The server also includes 236 GB of local storage and supports premium storage configurations, though it lacks dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The server achieves top-tier single-core results, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2342.0 and strong single-threaded compression performance, including 34.72 GB/sec in gzip compression and 14.32 GB/sec in bzip3 decompression. Conversely, multi-core and parallel processing capabilities are limited, with stress-ng multi-core performance and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom decile and bottom quartile respectively. Memory bandwidth also shows constraints, with large-block uncached read and write speeds registering in the bottom 10% of benchmarks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX4-2mds_v2 offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads that do not require extensive parallel CPU scaling. By bundling 84.0 GB of RAM with only 2 vCPUs, it allows operators to run memory-intensive applications without paying for unnecessary compute cores. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive single-threaded applications, text processing tasks, lightweight database operations with low concurrent query volumes, and caching systems that fit within the 236 GB local storage limit.
description: Microsoft Azure FX4-2mds_v2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 1 physical core and 2 vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 84.0 GB of RAM, a 260 MB L3 cache, and 236 GB of local storage. Benchmark results highlight a significant divergence in performance: the instance delivers top-tier single-core execution, as demonstrated by a Geekbench single-core score of 2342.0 and strong single-threaded compression throughput. However, its multi-core capabilities and large-block memory bandwidth are weak due to the single-core physical hardware constraint. This configuration provides a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that require minimal parallel processing. It is best suited for memory-bound single-threaded applications, text processing, and low-concurrency database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX4-2mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, 2 vCPUs, 84.0 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-bound tasks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX4-2mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 2 vCPUs, 84.0 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage, built for single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server pairing strong single-core Intel Xeon performance with an exceptional memory-to-vCPU ratio and local storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 2 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
84.0 GB of RAM providing an extreme 42.0 GB per core memory density
236 GB of local storage with premium storage capabilities
Top-tier single-core benchmark performance and strong text processing
Suited for memory-intensive, low-concurrency, or single-threaded workloads
